title: Google Launches Gemini 3.5 Flash at I/O 2026: 4x Faster Than Rivals, Now Powers AI Search
summary: Google unveiled Gemini 3.5 Flash at I/O 2026 — its first model combining frontier capability with low latency — instantly becoming the default for Gemini and Google Search worldwide.
content: Google introduced Gemini 3.5 Flash at Google I/O 2026 as its first model combining frontier-level capability with low-latency execution, shipping as the immediate default in the Gemini app and AI Mode in Google Search worldwide on launch day. The model outperforms Gemini 3.1 Pro across virtually all internal benchmarks — with especially strong gains on complex coding tasks — while delivering four times faster output token generation than competing frontier models. Priced at $1.50/$9.00 per million tokens, Gemini 3.5 Flash is roughly 40% cheaper than Gemini 3.1 Pro, and leads key benchmarks including Terminal-Bench 2.1 (76.2%) and multimodal understanding (CharXiv: 84.2%). Google also disclosed that it now processes over 3.2 quadrillion tokens monthly, up from 480 trillion just a year ago — a nearly sevenfold increase reflecting the rapid scaling of AI usage across its platforms. A more capable Gemini 3.5 Pro is already confirmed and will begin rolling out in June 2026.

title: Jury Unanimously Dismisses Elon Musk's $150 Billion Lawsuit Against OpenAI
summary: A federal jury in Oakland took less than two hours to throw out all of Elon Musk's claims against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, ruling the case was filed too late.
content: A nine-person federal jury in Oakland, California unanimously dismissed Elon Musk's $150 billion lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman on May 18–19, 2026, after deliberating for less than two hours following an eleven-day trial. Musk alleged that OpenAI had breached its charitable charter by abandoning its nonprofit mission, seeking Altman's removal and the company's reconversion back to nonprofit status. The jury found that the statute of limitations barred Musk's breach-of-charitable-trust claim, determining he had known about OpenAI's for-profit shift since 2021 — years before filing suit in 2024. U.S. District Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers adopted the advisory verdict and formally dismissed the case, delivering a decisive legal victory for OpenAI three weeks after testimony began. Musk pledged to appeal to the Ninth Circuit, arguing the dismissal was procedural rather than a ruling on the merits, while Altman called the result a validation of OpenAI's mission.

title: NextEra Buys Dominion Energy for $67 Billion in History's Largest Utility Deal, Driven by AI Power Demand
summary: NextEra Energy announced a $67 billion all-stock acquisition of Dominion Energy — the largest US utility merger ever — creating a power giant explicitly built to supply AI data centers.
content: NextEra Energy announced on May 18, 2026 that it will acquire Virginia-based Dominion Energy in a $67 billion all-stock deal, the largest US utility acquisition in history and the largest energy-sector transaction since Exxon's $80 billion Mobil acquisition in 1998. The combined entity will command a $249 billion market cap and serve approximately 10 million customer accounts across Florida, Virginia, North Carolina, and South Carolina. The strategic driver is AI data center demand: Dominion powers Northern Virginia — home to the world's largest concentration of data centers — where hyperscalers like Microsoft, Google, Amazon, and Meta have pushed electricity demand to unprecedented levels. Virginia data centers will require 16.6 gigawatts of capacity in 2026, growing to more than 33 GW by 2030, exceeding total US data center power demand from just four years prior. Together the companies carry a 130-gigawatt construction backlog, positioning the merged utility to absorb an estimated $1.1 trillion in infrastructure investment over five years.

title: Microsoft Agent 365 Goes Generally Available With Enterprise AI Governance Controls
summary: Microsoft launched Agent 365 as a centralized governance platform for enterprise AI agents, available at $15/user/month or bundled in the new Microsoft 365 E7 suite at $99/user/month.
content: Microsoft released Agent 365 to general availability on May 1, 2026, providing enterprises with a dedicated control plane to observe, govern, and secure AI agents across their entire organization. Priced at $15 per user per month — or included in the new Microsoft 365 E7 bundle combining Copilot, security, identity, and governance for $99/user/month — Agent 365 manages AI agents whether they operate with delegated access or independent credentials. Key capabilities include discovering and blocking shadow AI agents on Windows devices and cloud platforms, applying policy-based controls through Microsoft Intune, and extending network security controls to risky agent connections. The platform integrates with third-party ecosystems including AWS Bedrock, Google Cloud, Adobe, Zendesk, and n8n, allowing enterprises to govern agents across mixed-vendor environments from a single pane of glass. The launch reflects the growing enterprise challenge of managing hundreds of autonomous AI agents operating across systems without centralized oversight, oversight that Intune and Defender can now extend to locally installed agent software.

title: EU Agrees to Simplify AI Act Rules, Extends High-Risk Compliance Deadline by Up to 16 Months
summary: The European Council and Parliament struck a provisional deal to streamline the EU AI Act, extending high-risk AI compliance timelines and reducing burdens on smaller companies ahead of the August 2026 enforcement date.
content: The Council presidency and European Parliament negotiators reached a provisional agreement in May 2026 to simplify and streamline parts of the EU AI Act, acting with urgency as high-risk AI provisions are due to enter into force on August 2, 2026. The deal, part of the broader 'Omnibus VII' legislative package, extends the timeline for applying high-risk AI system rules by up to 16 months — giving companies significantly more time to build compliant documentation, risk assessments, and human-oversight mechanisms. Small and mid-cap companies receive extended exemptions under the revised framework, and requirements are reduced in limited circumstances to ease the overall compliance burden. The agreement also expands the ability to process sensitive personal data for AI bias detection, a critical tool for companies trying to satisfy the Act's fairness requirements. The expedited agreement reflects growing pressure from European industry groups who warned that the original timeline would disadvantage EU companies competing against less-regulated US and Chinese AI rivals.

title: Anthropic Launches 'Dreaming' for Claude Agents — Self-Improving Memory Between Sessions
summary: Anthropic introduced a 'dreaming' capability that lets Claude Managed Agents review past sessions, curate their own memory, and continuously self-improve performance without human intervention.
content: Anthropic introduced 'dreaming' — a scheduled memory-curation feature for Claude Managed Agents — on May 6, 2026, designed to help agents identify recurring patterns, eliminate outdated memories, and carry useful insights forward into future sessions. Instead of storing raw interactions indefinitely, the dreaming system reviews prior sessions, merges duplicate information, removes stale entries, and distills recurring patterns such as repeated mistakes or team workflow preferences into structured 'playbooks' that future sessions can reference. The model itself does not change through dreaming — no weight updates occur — making the entire learning process observable and auditable via plain-text notes. Early results have been striking: legal AI company Harvey reported approximately a 6x increase in task completion rates after implementing the dreaming feature on its Claude-based agents. Anthropic released the capability in research preview, noting it addresses one of the hardest challenges in deploying long-running AI agents: enabling them to learn from experience across thousands of sessions without becoming error-prone or operating with stale context.
